claim: Helminths interfere with immune system control of coinfections by microbial pathogens 16.
evidence_sentences: We show that mice infected with the intestinal helminth Nippostrongylus brasiliensis (Nb) exhibit a transitory impairment of resistance to airborne Mtb infection.
Furthermore, a second dose of Nb infection substantially increases the bacterial burden in the lungs of co-infected mice.
The Th2 response can thus enhance the intracellular persistence of Mtb, in part by mediating the alternative activation of macrophages via the IL-4Rα signaling pathway.
